2604.11193.title: TRACE: An Experiential Framework for Coherent Multi-hop Knowledge Graph Question Answering
2604.11193.paper_summary: Multi-hop Knowledge Graph Question Answering (KGQA) requires coherent reasoning across relational paths, yet existing methods often treat each reasoning step independently and fail to effectively leverage experience from prior explorations, leading to fragmented reasoning and redundant exploration. To address these challenges, we propose Trajectoryaware Reasoning with Adaptive Context and Exploration priors (TRACE), an experiential framework that unifies LLM-driven contextual reasoning with exploration prior integration to enhance the coherence and robustness of multihop KGQA. Specifically, TRACE dynamically translates evolving reasoning paths into natural language narratives to maintain semantic continuity, while abstracting prior exploration trajectories into reusable experiential priors that capture recurring exploration patterns. A dualfeedback re-ranking mechanism further integrates contextual narratives with exploration priors to guide relation selection during reasoning. Extensive experiments on multiple KGQA benchmarks demonstrate that TRACE consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines.
2604.11193.paper_summary_zh: 多跳知識圖譜問答（KGQA）需要在關聯路徑上進行連貫的推理，然而現有的方法通常將每個推理步驟獨立對待，未能有效利用先前探索的經驗，導致推理片段化和冗餘探索。為了解決這些挑戰，我們提出了具有自適應上下文和探索先驗的軌跡感知推理（TRACE），這是一個將基於大型語言模型的上下文推理與探索先驗整合的經驗框架，以增強多跳KGQA的連貫性和穩健性。具體而言，TRACE動態地將不斷演變的推理路徑轉換為自然語言敘述，以保持語義連貫性，同時將先前的探索軌跡抽象為可重用的經驗先驗，以捕捉重複的探索模式。一個雙重反饋重排序機制進一步將上下文敘述與探索先驗整合，以指導推理過程中的關係選擇。在多個KGQA基準上的廣泛實驗表明，TRACE始終超越了最先進的基準。
2604.11193.author: Yingxu Wang et.al.
2604.11193.authors: Yingxu Wang, Jiaxin Huang, Mengzhu Wang, Nan Yin
